body: [{"ac":1,"text":"Under policy=default with no --model pin, a claude subscription harness produces multiple candidates in routing_decision.candidates: one per available tier (at minimum opus-4.7 and sonnet-4.6; haiku if catalog has it). Verified by integration test that dispatches a default-policy request and asserts \u003e1 claude candidate.","kind":"prose","verifiable":false},{"ac":2,"text":"Each per-tier candidate carries the correct power and cost from internal/modelcatalog/catalog/models.yaml (sonnet=8, opus=10), and each routes to the same harness (claude), not via fiz/openrouter fallback.","kind":"prose","verifiable":false},{"ac":3,"text":"For a 2000-3000-token implementation prompt at role=implementer with policy=default, sonnet-4.6 beats opus-4.7 on score under default scoring weights (cost_input_per_m: 3 vs 15 dominates the cost component). Verified by a deterministic scoring test or a snapshot of the routing_decision event with the chosen tier asserted.","kind":"prose","verifiable":false},{"ac":4,"text":"Pinning --model sonnet-4.6 against the claude subscription harness routes through the claude harness (not fiz/openrouter). Verified by a test that sets up a claude harness mock and asserts dispatch destination.","kind":"prose","verifiable":false},{"ac":5,"text":"The routing_decision event for any excluded same-harness tier carries an explicit filter_reason — silent absence of a candidate row is forbidden when the tier is known to the catalog. Verified by negative test: artificially break sonnet-4.6's catalog metadata and assert it appears with filter_reason:\"power_missing\" (or similar) rather than vanishing.","kind":"negative","verifiable":true},{"ac":6,"text":"cd /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau \u0026\u0026 go test ./... green.","kind":"build-gate","verifiable":true},{"ac":7,"text":"No regression in existing routing tests (service_adr005_test.go, service_routing_test.go, internal/routing tests).","kind":"prose","verifiable":false}]

Verified by integration test that dispatches a default-policy request and asserts \u003e1 claude candidate.","coverage":"child 'auto-route: emit one candidate per supported tier for multi-tier subscription harnesses' AC1 (TestAutoRouteEnumeratesPerTierForMultiTierHarness)"},{"parent_ac":"2. Each per-tier candidate carries the correct power and cost from internal/modelcatalog/catalog/models.yaml (sonnet=8, opus=10), and each routes to the same harness (claude), not via fiz/openrouter fallback.","coverage":"child 'auto-route: emit one candidate per supported tier for multi-tier subscription harnesses' AC2 (TestPerTierCandidateCarriesCatalogPowerAndCost) and AC3 (TestPerTierCandidateRoutesViaSameHarness)"},{"parent_ac":"3. For a 2000-3000-token implementation prompt at role=implementer with policy=default, sonnet-4.6 beats opus-4.7 on score under default scoring weights (cost_input_per_m: 3 vs 15 dominates the cost component). Verified by a deterministic scoring test or a snapshot of the routing_decision event with the chosen tier asserted.","coverage":"child 'auto-route: cost-aware tier scoring picks cheapest tier meeting power_hint_fit' AC1 (TestSonnetBeatsOpusForImplementerPrompt) and AC2 (TestImplementerScoreComponentsCostDominates)"},{"parent_ac":"4. Pinning --model sonnet-4.6 against the claude subscription harness routes through the claude harness (not fiz/openrouter). Verified by a test that sets up a claude harness mock and asserts dispatch destination.","coverage":"child 'auto-route: --model pin prefers configured subscription harness over fiz/openrouter fallback' AC1 (TestModelPinPrefersSubscriptionHarnessOverOpenrouter) and AC2 (TestModelPinFallsBackWhenNoSubscriptionHarnessAdvertisesModel)"},{"parent_ac":"5. The routing_decision event for any excluded same-harness tier carries an explicit filter_reason — silent absence of a candidate row is forbidden when the tier is known to the catalog. Verified by negative test: artificially break sonnet-4.6's catalog metadata and assert it appears with filter_reason:\"power_missing\" (or similar) rather than vanishing.","coverage":"child 'auto-route: emit explicit filter_reason for excluded same-harness tiers (no silent drop)' AC1 (TestExcludedTierEmitsFilterReason), AC2 (TestNoSilentDropForCatalogKnownTier), AC3 (TestFilterReasonStringsAreStable)"},{"parent_ac":"6. cd /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau \u0026\u0026 go test ./... green.","coverage":"all four children include 'cd /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau \u0026\u0026 go test ./... green' as an AC gate"},{"parent_ac":"7. No regression in existing routing tests (service_adr005_test.go, service_routing_test.go, internal/routing tests).","coverage":"all four children include the no-regression gate naming service_adr005_test.go, service_routing_test.go, and internal/routing tests, plus lefthook run pre-commit"}],"child_ids":["fizeau-8ebac59d","fizeau-47a14e1e","fizeau-84b485d2","fizeau-d37752b9"],"rationale":"Split along the four independent failure modes the parent bundles: (1) per-tier candidate enumeration under a multi-tier harness — the structural fix that unblocks everything else; (2) cost-aware scoring that selects sonnet over opus once both are enumerated — depends on (1) but is a distinct behavior with its own test surface; (3) --model pin destination preference, a separate routing bug that exists independent of policy=default and would not be fixed by (1)+(2) alone (pinning today still falls back to openrouter); (4) explicit filter_reason emission for excluded same-harness tiers, a diagnostic-correctness fix touching the event emitter, orthogonal to the routing path. Each child is independently testable with named Test* symbols, preserves all parent non-scope items (openrouter 401, DDx-side changes, catalog metadata), carries the github-issue-6 cross-ref, and includes go test + lefthook gates. Child 2 depends on child 1 landing first, declared explicitly in its AC3. ac-quality:needs-refinement label intentionally dropped from children because the splits sharpen the ACs to named Test* symbols; impact:cost retained on the two children where cost is the direct subject."}
